name: Trackio
tagline: Local-first experiment tracking that drops in for wandb
seoTitle: Trackio - Local Experiment Tracking by Hugging Face
metaDescription: Trackio is a free, local-first experiment tracker from Hugging Face. API-compatible with wandb, it stores runs in SQLite with a built-in dashboard.
overview: Trackio is a free experiment tracking library from Hugging Face for logging metrics, media, and tables from machine learning runs. It is local-first: the dashboard runs on your machine by default and logs are stored in a SQLite database, so you don't need to create an account to start tracking.

Its API mirrors wandb. You can write `import trackio as wandb` and keep your existing `init`, `log`, and `finish` calls, which makes it a drop-in replacement for teams already using that logging style. A Gradio-inspired dashboard ships with it for viewing metrics and media.

As an experiment-tracking tool, Trackio fits both human and agent-driven workflows. Because runs live in SQLite, it includes a CLI for querying data (including read-only SQL), which is handy for LLM-driven analysis and high-throughput parallel experiments. You can optionally push runs to a free Hugging Face Space for collaboration.
features: API-compatible with wandb.init, wandb.log, and wandb.finish, so existing logging code works with `import trackio as wandb`
Local-first by default: runs are stored in a SQLite database on your machine, no account required
Built-in Gradio-inspired dashboard to view metrics, media, tables, and alerts
CLI for programmatic access, including read-only SQL queries over your run data
Cloud-optional: pass a space_id to log to a free Hugging Face Space for collaborative experiments
Forkable design — Python backend and API with a Svelte 5 dashboard you can extend or replace

gettingStarted.intro: Trackio requires Python 3.10 or higher. Install it, log some metrics, then open the dashboard.

steps.heading: Install Trackio
steps.body: Install from PyPI with pip (or uv).

steps.heading: Log metrics from your training loop
steps.body: Initialize a project, log metrics each step, and finish the run. The API matches wandb, so you can also write `import trackio as wandb`.

steps.heading: Launch the dashboard
steps.body: View your runs locally in the browser. Pass a project name to open it directly.

useCases: Track loss and accuracy across local training runs without signing up for a hosted service
Migrate off wandb with minimal code changes by aliasing trackio as wandb
Let LLMs or agents analyze experiment results by running read-only SQL queries over the SQLite store
Push local runs to a free Hugging Face Space to share results with collaborators
